knowledge_need: Study topic as Engineer-Philosopher. Attempt 1 of up to 3. Give compact Builder-relevant understanding, validation needs, and return plan.

part_digests.name: core meaning
part_digests.meaning: An atom is a minimal reusable learning unit; a case pattern is a recurring situation shape with context, triggers, actions, and outcomes. Builder learning needs both but must not confuse durable general knowledge with one episode cluster.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Defines the central distinction needed before deciding what should be stored, retrieved, reused, or promoted.
compact_missing_knowledge: Repo-specific acceptance criteria for atom candidates
What evidence separates reusable atom from repeated case memory
How current Builder retrieval represents context
compact_safe_learning_steps: Compare accepted atom examples against case-like examples read-only
Map each item to trigger, invariant, action, expected delta
compact_validation_needed: A reviewer or validator can classify examples consistently as atom, case pattern, or open gap
The classification improves a later decision without adding false certainty
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X as a classification rule: atom equals reusable invariant; case pattern equals contextual recurrence.

part_digests.name: why I am not ideal
part_digests.meaning: The weakness is premature abstraction: I may compress a rich case into a fake atom, or keep a true invariant trapped inside bulky case history.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Shows why Builder needs acceptance gates and behavior-delta proof before treating learned material as durable intelligence.
compact_missing_knowledge: Failure examples where abstraction harmed Builder decisions
Whether retrieval currently favors labels over tested behavior
compact_safe_learning_steps: Prefer draft labels until tested against new cases
compact_validation_needed: At least one counterexample test does not break the proposed atom
A case pattern remains demotable if it fails outside its original context
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X with a caution: learning quality depends on resisting both over-compression and under-generalization.

part_digests.name: builder relevance
part_digests.meaning: For self-build or child-agent build, atoms support compact retention and fast decision reuse, while case patterns support diagnosis, transfer, and training examples.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Connects the distinction to Builder architecture: storage, retrieval, reuse, and behavior delta proof.
compact_missing_knowledge: Which Builder modules consume atoms versus cases
How child-agent lessons inherit or reject parent patterns
compact_safe_learning_steps: Route atom candidates to compact storage only after validation
Keep case patterns as evidence bundles until generalized
Record intended downstream use for each learned item
compact_validation_needed: Retrieved learning changes a real or simulated decision in the intended direction
The same learning does not trigger in unrelated situations
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X as an operational split: atoms feed durable decision reuse; cases feed diagnosis and future atom discovery.

part_digests.name: validation
part_digests.meaning: Understanding is validated only when the atom/case distinction predicts useful handling: storage shape, retrieval trigger, promotion gate, and behavior effect.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Prevents the topic from becoming vocabulary without proof of better Builder behavior.
compact_missing_knowledge: Existing validators for accepted atom retention
compact_safe_learning_steps: Use small read-only classification tests first
Then run bounded validator or owner-run proof if available
compact_validation_needed: Observed behavior delta tied to the retrieved learning
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X with proof requirements attached, not as accepted knowledge.

part_digests.name: promotion decision
part_digests.meaning: Promotion decides whether the learning remains a case pattern, becomes an atom candidate, or stays an open gap. Promotion should be evidence-based, reversible, and scoped.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Produces the parent task's actionable outcome without pretending the draft is already accepted.
compact_missing_knowledge: Canonical schema for atom candidate metadata
compact_safe_learning_steps: Classify as case pattern by default when context is still essential
Promote to atom candidate only when invariant and reuse target are clear
Keep open gap when evidence is ambiguous
compact_validation_needed: Promotion record cites evidence, scope, trigger, and expected behavior delta
A failed validation can demote or quarantine the candidate
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X as a three-way decision: case pattern, atom candidate, or open gap.

dependencies: X2 depends on X1 because failure modes require the atom/case distinction
X3 depends on X1 because relevance changes by storage and reuse type
X5 depends on X4 because promotion needs validation evidence
duplicates_or_overlaps: X4 overlaps X5 because promotion is a validation outcome
X1 overlaps X3 because meaning is partly architectural
can_skip_for_now: None; X5 can be deferred if no promotion authority or validator exists

parent_return_plan.how_to_rebuild_x: Define the atom/case distinction, identify the Builder weakness it addresses, map relevance to storage and decision reuse, attach validation gates, then return a conservative promotion decision.
parent_return_plan.next_small_action: Create a read-only classification checklist for known learning examples: invariant, context, trigger, action, expected delta, evidence, decision.
proof_needed: At least one held-out case tests the proposed distinction
A retrieved accepted item causes measurable decision reuse without false activation

promotion_decision.owner_decision_required: only if promotion beyond case pattern is requested
promotion_decision.reason: Batch source answer is material only; default is compact case pattern with part map and parent return plan.
